Used B y copywriTers
and Their clienTs

-- 4 of 26 --

Contracts 101 	Page 
Introduction
If you’re a beginning copywriter, understanding the kinds of contracts you’ll be asked to
sign with major mailers is important.
We have included four sample contracts in this report to give you a general idea of the
kinds of agreements copywriters make with their clients.
These contracts are offered as examples only and are not intended to be legal advice
of any kind. It is strongly recommended that copywriters have legal counsel review all
agreements prior to signing them.
The names of clients, products and other pertinent details have been deleted in accor-
dance with confidentiality agreements.
Wendy Makepeace
Wendy Makepeace
General Manager
The Total Package

-- 5 of 26 --

Contracts 101 	Page 
–Part One–
Simple Agreement For
Copywriting Services

-- 6 of 26 --

Contracts 101 	Page 
Agreement for Copywriting Services
Date: (xxxx)
Copywriter: (Name)
Job Description: New Direct Mail Package
Project Timeline: (xxxx)
Copywriting terms/payments:
$25,000 advance against $0.05/piece royalty on all names mailed (house and outside). $12,500 of
the advance to be paid at the signing of this agreement to ensure the writer’s availability. The remain-
ing $12,500 advance will be paid upon approval of final copy.
Copywriter will provide cover and lead-in tests at no additional charge once package becomes the
control. In the unlikely event that the package is unsuccessful but worthy of retesting with revisions,
copywriter will revise copy for retest at no additional charge.
Copywriter will provide references to studies that support all claims in the copy if the studies are not
referenced in the (xxxx) previously published material. If writer is proposing any new premiums, the
creative brief/outline must indicate where the content will be drawn from. The first copy draft must
then include everything we need to begin writing any new premiums, including all promises it must
fulfill, the source we will draw from to fulfill each promise, including each specific reference or page-
number. (We will provide you with a “start kit” containing any of (xxxx) newsletters and premiums that
you might find helpful, previous acquisition packages and creative analysis, market research, and
access to (xxxx) website.)
Additional Bonuses:
Should we choose to invest in online testing of this package, copywriter will consult with us on how to
best leverage the message online and copy chief initial dedicated emails or other online tests. Copy-
writer will receive a $5,000 bonus each time the online magalog generates $100,000 in new online
sales (outside of any offline-to-online efforts).
Confidentiality/Copyright terms:
Copywriter agrees not to disclose any information of (xxxx) or any of its affiliates, including but not
limited to content, mailing results and strategy. All copyrights are property of client. Copywriter ac-
knowledges and agrees that the Work will be a “Work Made for Hire” as defined by the United States
Copyright Act with Client as the author. If the Work is not, for any reason, held to be a “Work Made for
Hire,” Copywriter hereby assigns to
Client for its own use and that of its successors and assigns, all of its right, title and interest in the
Work. Copywriter further agrees to execute any document necessary to effectuate this assignment to
Client For purposes of this Agreement, “Work” includes all of the original matter created by Copywriter
for direct mail packages. Nothing in this agreement should be construed as creating an employee-
employer relationship. Please sign and return this agreement to (xxxx) via facsimile at (xxxx) or by
mail to the letterhead address. Send invoices for the “Amount Due” above to (xxxx) along with your
Social Security number or Federal ID number.

-- 7 of 26 --

Contracts 101 	Page 
Client Signed: 	____________________
Client Dated: 	____________________
Agency Signed: 	____________________
Agency Dated: 	____________________

-- 8 of 26 --

Contracts 101 	Page 
–Part Two–
In Depth Agreement For
Copywriting Services

-- 9 of 26 --

Contracts 101 	Page 10
COPYWRITER AGREEMENT
This Agreement this - (xx) day of (xx) (year) is between (xxxx) a limited liability company doing busi-
ness in (xxxx) (herein “LLC”) and (xxxx), residing at (xxxx) (herein “Writer”). LLC and Writer shall be
mutually referred to herein as the “Parties”.
RECITALS
LLC publishes and markets various newsletters, including (xxxx) (herein, the “Newsletter”).
LLC wishes to have Writer as an independent contractor responsible for writing a package (herein,
the “Work”) that will market the Newsletter to the public. In exchange for the mutual consideration
contained herein between LLC and Writer, it is hereby agreed as follows:
Writer’s Performance.
Writer agrees to produce the Work as directed by LLC. Work shall be delivered according
to the following due schedule: a first draft due (xxxx) (herein, “First Draft”), and the final
revised copy due (xxxx) (herein, “Final Copy”). Writer agrees to make revisions and/or, if
necessary, a full re-write of the Work so that it shall meet such standards, length and quality
as required by LLC.
Writer agrees to provide the following back-up along with submission of the Work:
sources of all factually material claims made in the Work,
dates of books, if any, that the Work refers to, and
citations to any studies, editorial and/or other material sources the Work refers to, in suf-
ficient detail to permit material fact checking of the Work.
Compensation.
In exchange for Writer’s perfomance as described in Section 1 above, LLC shall compensate
him according to the following schedule:
Twelve Thousand, Five Hundred Dollars ($12,500.00) upfront upon execution of this Agree-
ment.
Twelve Thousand, Five Hundred Dollars ($12,500.00) upon receipt and approval of the
Final Copy.
If LLC decides in its sole discretion, to use the Work following its initial test mailing, Copy-
writer shall be compensated a royalty of five Cents ($0.05) for every piece mailed to the pub-
lic (herein “Royalty”). Royalty shall be paid every time Work is used at this rate until LLC decides
in its sole discretion to cease use of the Work.
If LLC decides in its sole discretion that the First Draft is inappropriate, this Agreement shall
terminate immediately and Writer may keep the initial payment of Twelve Thousand, Five Hun-
dred Dollars ($12,500.00) and forgo any and all future payment.
Independent Contractor.
LLC and Writer jointly and severally agree that the relationship of Writer to LLC is that of an indepen-
A.
B.
1.
a.
b.
i.
ii.
iii.
2.
a.
b.
c.
3.

-- 10 of 26 --

Contracts 101 	Page 11
dent contractor, and that in no event shall this Agreement be deemed to have created a partnership, joint
venture or employee-employer relationship. LLC shall have no control of the details of where, how and
when Writer performs his services and functions under Section 1 of this Agreement.
Assignment of Copyrights.
IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties hereto have duly executed this Agreement as of the date
first written above.
It is further agreed that LLC shall be the owner to the fullest extent permitted under law of all
intellectual property produced under this Agreement and associated with the Work. Writer
hereby transfers full ownership of any copyrights of his/hers produced under this Agreement to
LLC, including, but not limited to, the exclusive rights throughout the world to first publish the
Work, to republish/reproduce and authorize the republication/reproduction thereof in English or
in translation, and in any and all media including electronic media now existing or to be discov-
ered in the future (i.e. Internet, world wide web, etc.). Writer, upon LLC’s request, shall execute
any documents to perfect and/or register such an assignment.
Non-Disclosure.
Writer also acknowledges that information and techniques obtained by Writer from LLC in
preparing the Work is proprietary and confidential and Writer covenants not to disclose. It is
further agreed that LLC shall be the owner to the fullest extent permitted under law of all intel-
lectual property produced under this Agreement and associated with the Work. Writer hereby
transfers full ownership of any copyrights of his/hers produced under this Agreement to LLC,
including, but not limited to, the exclusive rights throughout the world to first publish the Work,
to republish/reproduce and authorize the republication/reproduction thereof in English or in
translation, and in any and all media including electronic media now existing or to be discov-
ered in the future (i.e. Internet, world wide web, etc.). Writer, upon LLC’s request, shall execute
any documents to perfect and/or register such an assignment.
Non-Disclosure.
Writer also acknowledges that informati on and techniques obtained by Writer from LLC in
preparing the Work is proprietary and confidenti al and Writer covenants not to disclose it to a
third party, provide it to a competi tor of LLC, or to use it on his behalf or that of any third party.
Assignment of Agreement.
Writer’s services and functi ons are considered unique. This Agreement and/or any rights or
obligati ons herein may not be assigned or otherwise transferred by Writer to any other party
without the prior writt en consent of LLC.
Integration, Amendments & Modifications.
This Agreement embodies the enti re agreement between the parties hereto with respect to the
subject matt er herein. This Agreement may not be amended or modifi ed except by a writi ng
duly executed by the parties hereto.
Choice of Law.
This Agreement shall be construed in accordance with and governed by the internal laws of the
State of (xxxx) regardless of any conflicts of law. The forum for any dispute shall be limited to
4.
5.
6.
7.
8.
9.

-- 11 of 26 --

Contracts 101 	Page 1
courts within the State of (xxxx). Writer expressly consents to personal jurisdicti on of (xxxx).
Facsimile Signature.
Parties agree that a facsimile signature shall be deemed an original.
Interpretation.
Each Party has participated in the drafting of this Agreement and was presented with the opportunity
to confer with counsel of its choice.
Client Signed: 	____________________
Client Dated: 	____________________
Agency Signed: 	____________________
Agency Dated: 	____________________
10.
11.

-- 12 of 26 --

Contracts 101 	Page 1
–Part Three–
Contract For
Marketing Consultation

-- 13 of 26 --

Contracts 101 	Page 1
MARKETING AGREEMENT
THIS AGREEMENT is made on the (xxxx) day of (xxxx), (xxxx) by and between (xxxx), doing busi-
ness as (xxxx) (the “Client”) which has an address of (xxxx) and (xxxx) doing business as (xxxx) (the
“Agency”) which has an address of (xxxx).
RECITALS
NOW THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual covenants and agreement contained herein, the
parties agree that Agency shall provide the Client with certain advertising services for (xxxx) and
other related products which include marketing consulting, sales strategies and copywriting (the “work
product”) for a fee based on formulas outlined herein. To that extent, both parties acknowledge that
no assets will be developed during the duration of this Agreement. More specifically, the Agency and
the Client make the following agreements:
Independent Contractor Confidentiality Agreement: Agency agrees to execute an INDEPENDENT
CONTRACTORS CONFIDENTIALITY AGREEMENT with the Client.
Subject Matter: Agency shall provide its work product in conjunction with the Client for the purpose
of marketing (xxx).
Compensation: As compensation for creative marketing services provided by the Agency, the Cli-
ent agrees to pay Agency as follows:
Direct Mail Promotion of Products to Non-Customers:
For each direct mail promotion created by the Agency for the purpose of selling a product to
Non-Customer (i.e., a person who is not currently a customer of the Client), it will receive a
$25,000 advance against royalties of $0.03 per copy of the promotion that is ultimately deliv-
ered to prospects ($30 per 1,000 pieces mailed).
The advance against royalties will be treated as follows:
A payment of $12,500 will be paid to Agency upon the Client’s reservation of the Agency’s
time to create each promotion.
The balance of$12,500 will be paid to Agency upon delivery of First Draft copy. Should a
project be cancelled by client after First Draft Copy is delivered, the entire $25,000 advance
paid to Agency will be forfeited by the Client as a “kill fee.”
The Client may mail up to 833,333 copies of each promotion without additional payment to
Agency. The quantity is covered by the $25,000 advance. After the first 833,333 pieces of a
promotion are distributed, Agency will be paid $0.03 ($30 per thousand) on all additional cop-
ies of that promotion that are delivered to prospects.
Should the promotion or any substantial part thereof that was originally prepared by Agency for
the purpose of attracting new customers be adapted and used to promote a product to existing
customers, Agency will be paid 10% gross revenues generated by the promotion, minus direct
fulfillment costs and direct marketing costs.
For the purposes of this entire agreement, “direct fulfillment costs” and “direct marketing costs”
are defined as follows:
Direct marketing costs are defined as the direct cost of printing, media (mail list rental or
A.
B.
C.
D.
1.
2.
a.
b.
3.
4.
a.

-- 14 of 26 --

Contracts 101 	Page 1
media buys), postage and lettershop.
Direct fulfillment costs include the direct costs associated with order acceptance and pro-
cessing including call center and credit card processing fees.
Should the promotion or any substantial part thereof intended for use in one medium (i.e. direct
mail, print, the internet, TV or radio) be adapted for use in a print ad or ad in any other medium,
Agency shall be paid the greater of: 1) 10% of gross sales, minus direct marketing and fulfill-
ment costs, or 2) 10% of media expenditures.
For the purposes of this entire agreement, “media expenditures” are defined as follows:
Media Expenditures are defined as the cost incurred by the client to print, mail or other-
wise place the promotion before prospective customers in direct mail, printed publications,
magazines, newspapers, radio, television or internet or other medium.
Should Agency and Client agree to engage a subcontractor, such as another copywriter or
graphic artist, Client shall be solely responsible for the flat-fee payment due to such subcon-
tractor. Should Agency so desire, it may from time to time, share a portion of its royalties with
such subcontractors.
Promotions to Non-Customers In Other Media:
For each non-direct-mail promotion to non-customers (i.e. promotions delivered to non- cus-
tomers through the Internet, television, radio, magazines, newspapers and other print publica-
tions, or any other medium) Agency will be paid the greater of: 1) 10% of gross sales, minus
direct marketing and fulfillment costs, or 2) 10% of media expenditures.
Should Agency and Client agree to engage a subcontractor, such as another copywriter or
graphic artist, Client shall be solely responsible for the flat-fee payment due to such subcon-
tractors. Should Agency so desire, it may from time to time, share a portion of its royalties with
such subcontractors.
Promotions of Products to Existing Customers:
For each promotion created by the Agency for the purpose of selling additional products to
existing customers, the Client shall pay the Agency 10% of gross revenues, minus direct mar-
keting and fulfillment costs, as defined above.
Should a promotion or any substantial part thereof that was originally prepared by Agency for
the purpose of selling a product to existing customers be adapted and used to attract new
customers, Agency will be paid the greater of: 1) 10% of gross revenues generated by the
promotion, minus direct marketing and fulfillment costs, or 2) A royalty equal to 10% of the cost
incurred by the client to print, mail, or otherwise place the promotion before prospective cus-
tomers.
Should any part of a promotion intended for use in one medium (i.e. direct mail, print, the
Internet, TV or radio) be adapted for use in a print ad or ad in any other medium, Agency shall
be paid the greater of: 1) 10% of gross sales, minus direct marketing and fulfillment costs, or 2)
10% of media expenditures.
Should Agency and Client agree to engage a subcontractor, such as another copywriter or
graphic artist, Client shall be solely responsible for the flat-fee payment due to such subcon-
tractors. Should Agency so desire, it may from time to time, share a portion of its royalties with
b.
5.
a.
6.
E.
1.
2.
F.
1.
2.
3.
4.

-- 15 of 26 --

Contracts 101 	Page 1
such subcontractors.
Payment Procedures:
Promotions to Non-Customers: Client will produce a report specifying the final quantities and
royalties due for each planned promotion prior to the release of each promotion to prospects.
Promotions to Existing Customers: Client will produce a weekly report specifying the gross rev-
enue, direct product and marketing costs, commissionable revenues and the commissions due
the Agency as a result of promotions the Agency produces for the Client.
Monthly remittance: All compensation due Agency will be remitted on the 15th of the month fol-
lowing the month in which the compensation was earned.
Expenses:
The following are expenses for which the Agency is responsible: All expenses incurred in the
performance of its obligation to the Client, including but not limited to office rental, utilities,
long-distance telephone, salaries, subscriptions and royalty compensation of outside copywrit-
ers.
The following are expenses for which the Client is responsible: All travel expenses incurred by
the Agency at the Client’s specific request, including airfare, rental cars, and meals for Agency
employees and independent contractors hired and retained by Agency.
Hours: The Agency is required to commit a reasonable number of hours during a week necessary
to provide the services agreed to between the parties during the term of this agreement. Given the
level of expertise of the Agency’s principal, (xxxx), and the time sensitive nature of certain publica-
tion deadlines, those services shall be provided substantially by (xxxx).
Effective Date: (xxxx)
Notices: Any notice, consent, request, claim or other communication hereunder shall be deemed
to have been duly given if delivered or mailed by registered or certified mail, return receipt re-
quested, to the following addresses:
(xxxx)
(xxxx)
(xxxx)
(xxxx)
Any party may change such address by notice given in the manner provided above.
Attorney’s Fees: In the event either party to this agreement shall employ legal counsel to protect
its rights under this agreement or to enforce any term or provision of this agreement, then the
party prevailing in any such legal action shall be entitled to recover from the other party all of its
reasonable attorney’s fees, costs, and expenses incurred in relation to such claim.
Entire Agreement: This agreement, together with all exhibits and the documents referred to herein,
contains all the terms and conditions agreed upon by the parties with respect to marketing copy-
writing and strategies, and shall not be amended or modified except by written instrument signed
by all of the parties.
G.
1.
2.
3.
H.
1.
2.
I.
J.
K.
L.
M.

-- 16 of 26 --

Contracts 101 	Page 1
Binding Effect on Representatives and Successors: This agreement shall be binding upon and
inure to the benefit of the representatives, heirs, estates, successors and assigns to the parties.
Copies: This agreement may be executed simultaneously in two or more counterparts, each of
which, shall be deemed original, but all of which shall constitute one and the same instrument.
Law Governing: This agreement shall be governed by and construed under the laws of the State
of (xxxx). Any dispute arising out of this agreement shall be adjudicated within the state in an ap-
propriate forum.
Right to Cancel: Parties shall have the right to cancel this agreement within ninety (90) days writ-
ten notice. In the event that the Client desires to terminate for cause for breach of the terms of
this agreement, the Agency shall be given thirty (30) days to cure or other reasonable period of
time, at Client’s discretion. In the event of cancellation of this agreement or the death or disability
of (xxxx) the Agency’s principal, Client is obligated to continue paying royalties as outlined in this
document on promotions created by Agency so long as they are used.
Term: This agreement is in effect until (xxxx) unless terminated pursuant to paragraph 17.
Termination for Breach: This agreement with the Agency may be terminated for cause. Cause may
be defined as, but not limited to, the following occurrences caused by the conduct of the Agency,
its employees, officers, or independent contractors: The conviction of any felony, or misdemeanor
which amounts to a crime involving the taking of property, in any domestic or foreign court; or the
conviction of the Agency of any regulatory violation whether by a federal regulatory agency or self-
regulatory agency.
Code of Ethics: By this agreement the Agency, all the Agency’s officers and personnel agree to
comply with the Client’s Code of Ethics that is then in effect.
Client accepts sole responsibility for the accuracy of all research and other background materials
it provides to the Agency for each promotion. Furthermore, Client accepts sole responsibility to en-
sure that all statements made by the Agency in each promotion are accurate, fully substantiated,
legally permissible and that their presentation complies with all laws and regulations established
by governing national, state and local authorities prior to the promotion being presented to pros-
pects or customers.
Should the Client and/or Client’s legal counsel determine that any statement included in draft copy
may be inaccurate, misleading or in violation of any governing law or regulation, the Client will
direct the Agency to make such changes as the Client and its legal counsel deem necessary to
bring the work product into compliance. Furthermore, should Client, on advice of legal counsel,
determine that additional disclaimers or other clarifying statements must be included in the sales
copy to fully satisfy legal and/or regulatory requirements, Client will direct Agency to include said
disclaimers or other statements. In all cases, Agency will make all required changes to the satis-
faction of the Client and its legal counsel.
The Client’s presentation of the Agency’s final work product to customers and/or prospects shall
be construed as the Client’s approval of the work product and Client’s confirmation that it has
found the promotion to be accurate, not misleading and in full compliance with all governing legal
and regulatory guidelines
Should presentation of Agency’s work product to customers or non-customers result in legal action
of any kind against Client, Client agrees to hold the Agency harmless.
N.
O.
P.
Q.
R.
S.
T.

-- 17 of 26 --

Contracts 101 	Page 1
The parties have executed this agreement as of the day and year first above written and attest to this
fact with the following signatures:
Client Signed: 	____________________
Client Dated: 	____________________
Agency Signed: 	____________________
Agency Dated: 	____________________

-- 18 of 26 --

Contracts 101 	Page 1
–Part Four–
Consulting
Agreement

-- 19 of 26 --

Contracts 101 	Page 0
CONSULTING AGREEMENT
AGREEMENT, dated this XXth day of (Month, Year) by and between (“Name”), and (“Client”) (colle-
tively the “Parties”).
WITNESSETH:
WHEREAS, (Client) is in the business of, (product) and providing facsimile, e-mail and is planning
to go into an e-commerce business; and WHEREAS, (Client), a wholly owned subsidiary of (Client),
has retained the services of (Name) to continue to assist (Client in furnishing copywriting and related
services to its health publications, dated (Date) (the “Consulting Agreement”); and WHEREAS, (Cli-
ent) desires to retain (Name) to provide additional consulting services net set forth in the Consulting
Agreement with (Client) to grow its various existing and future businesses; and WHEREAS, the par-
ties with to compensate (Name) for his anticipated contributions to the growth of (Client) businesses
until at least (Date); NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual promises, covenants and
undertakings set forth herein, and for other good and sufficient consideration, the value of which is
hereby acknowledged, (Name) and (product) hereby agree as follows:
SECTION 1
Term. The services contemplated by this Agreement shall commence on the date of execution by
both Parties and shall continue thereafter until (Date).
SECTION II
Consulting Services to be Provided. (Name) will perform consulting and advisory services on be-
half of (Client) with respect to its various businesses as requested by (Client). It is anticipated that
except for (Name)’s services required under (Name) Consulting Agreement with (Client) and the
completion of existing contractual and other obligations, as set forth in Exhibit A hereto, (Name)
will devote substantially full time to (Client). (Name) and/or Consultant can enter into or perform
new projects with entities’ other than (Client) so long as those new projects do not conflict with
either’s duties to (Client).
Place Where services to be Provided. It is anticipated that (Name) will perform his services at
(Address). Upon (Client) reasonable request, (Name) shall be available to render services at the
offices of (company” located at (address), or at such other place as (Client) may designate.
SECTION III
Independent Contractor Status. Both (Name) and (Client) agree that (Name) shall be acting as
an independent contractor and not as an employee, servant of agent of (Client). Accordingly, it is
agreed that (Name) shall not have any authority to act of or on behalf of (Client) or bind (Client)
without its express consent. (Name) shall not be considered as having employee status for any
purpose, including, inter alai, any employee benefit plan applicable to (Client) employees gener-
ally. (Name) is responsible for the payment of any taxes, including, without limitation, all federal,
state and local, income, capital gains, social security, unemployment and disability taxes, sales
and use taxes and other business taxes that may be due on stock options or monies received by
him under this Agreement. The election or appointment of (Name) as a director or (company) shall
not be deemed to make him an employee.
SECTION IV
A.
A.
B.
A.

-- 20 of 26 --

Contracts 101 	Page 1
Compensation
Option to Purchase an Interest in (Client).
(Client) agrees to grant (Name) an option to purchase 10% of (Client) stock, outstanding as
of the date hereof, for an amount computed as follows (the “Option”).
The price of the option is to be equal to 10% of the sum of two formulas which arrives at an
Adjusted Value for (Client), and adjusted for any actual or contingent claims, as hereinaf-
ter described. The first formula, derived by (Client) and its accounts, is for the subscription
portion of the business, which is computed to be ($Value), as set forth in Exhibit B attached
hereto. The actual numbers used to determine the exercise price of the option shall be de-
termined as at (Date).
The Adjusted Value for (Client) shall be subject to a reduction for any actual or potential
claims to the extent of 50% of the amount so accrued on the (client) balance sheet as at
(Date), plus the amount of estimated legal fees relating to the defense of any such claims
or claims. The amount of such estimated legal fees shall be set forth in writing by counsel.
In the event counsel to (Client) is unable to estimate or quantify the amount of the potential
liability for purposes of the (Client) balance sheet (Client) and (Name), in consultation with
that counsel, shall agree on the amount that should be deducted from the Adjusted Value of
(Client). This adjustment, including estimated legal fees, shall hereinafter be referred to as
“the possible loss contingency”.
The Parties acknowledge that these formulas being utilized to determine the purchase price
of the option and to compute a portion of the growth of (Client) is unrelated to generally ac-
cepted accounting principles (“GAAP”) or tax accounting.
The Option may be exercised in full on (Date), and vest and be payable in installments on
the dates and in the amounts set forth below as follows:
Date
Portion of Option Aggregate
Exercisable Exercise Price
Month XX, Year ¼ $000,000
Month XX, Year ½ $000,000
Month XX, Year The balance $000,000
Upon payment of the exercise price of each portion or for all of the shares subject to the
Option, (Name) shall be issued the certificate for the number of shares of the Common
Stock of (Client) which he purchases.
Vesting in the event of an IPO of (Client). In the event of an initial public offering or sale of (Cli-
ent) prior to (Date) and (Name) is acting as a consultant at that time, the entire Option shall
vest, in full, and become immediately exercisable, upon closing of a sale or initial public offer-
ing if this Agreement has not been previously terminated.
Partial Vesting. In the event (Name) is terminated as a consultant, as set forth in Section VIII
or voluntarily resigns while (Name) is acting as a consultant, then the proportionate part of the
A.
1.
a.
b.
c.
d.
e.
2.
3.

-- 21 of 26 --

Contracts 101 	Page 
Option based on the schedule set forth in Section A.1.f., shall be deemed vested.
Deferred Compensation. If the following conditions are met, the Option shall terminate and
(Client) shall pay deferred compensation to (Name) as set forth below (the “Deferred Compen-
sation”):
there is no closing of a sale of (Client) or the closing of initiial public offering of the securi-
ties of (Client) in the gross amount of at least $X million, prior to (Date): and
this Agreement is not terminated prior to the end of its term.
The option shall be canceled and in lieu thereof, (Name) shall be paid Deferred Compensation,
as set forth herein. However, to the extent (Name) has exercised the Option referred to in this
Section IV, then (Client) agrees to cancel those shares and repay (Name) the purchase price
paid, plus interest at the effect on the date of cancellation. Deferred Compensation shall be
equal to ten percent (10%) of the increase in value of (Client) between (Date) and (Date).
Calculation of the Deferred Compensation. The Deferred Compensation shall be valued in ac-
cordance with the formula set forth in Exhibit B.
Payment of Deferred Compensation. At (Clients’) option, the Deferred Compensation shall be
payable in cash within Thirty (30) days of (Date), or quarterly over the three (3) year period
following (Date), or quarterly over the three (3) year period following (Date), with interest at the
U.S. Treasury Bill rate published in the Wall Street Journal on January 1 of each applicable
year. The actual payment(s) of any deferred compensation due (Name) in a any one year shall
be limited to 25% of the financial statement line item “cash provided by operating activities” for
(Client) and subsidiaries for the prior fiscal year (“cash flow”). To the extent any deferred com-
pensation due (Name) exceeds 25% cash flow, it shall be paid in each subsequent year to the
25% cash flow limitation described above.
(Name)’s Representations and Warranties. (Name) hereby represents and warrants the following
to (Client):
No Violation. Neither the execution and delivery of this Agreement, nor the provision of ser-
vices as contemplated herein, is prohibited by, or is a violation of, any contract, agreement, law
judgment or court order to which (Name) is a party or by which (Name) is or may be bound.
Nor is the consent or approval of any person or entity not a party to this Agreement required.
Litigation/Disputes. There is no judicial, administrative or arbitral action, suit or investigation
or proceedings pending or to (Name)’s knowledge, threatened against (Name) which would
prevent performance of this Agreement by (Name).
No Reliance on Outside Statements or Documents. In entering into this Agreement, (Name)
has not relied upon any documents or statements, oral or written, by (Client) or any of its em-
ployees or agents, not contained or reflected in this Agreement, exclusive of dollar amounts set
forth or utilized in arriving at the dollar amounts used to obtain the exercise price.
SECTION VI
(Client’s) Representations and Warranties. (Client) hereby represents and warrants the following:
Organization and Standing. (Client) is a corporation duly organized, validly existing and in
good standing under the laws of the State of (State) and has the right, power, legal capacity
4.
a.
b.
5.
6.
B.
1.
2.
3.
A.
1.

-- 22 of 26 --

Contracts 101 	Page 
and authority to enter into, and perform its obligations under this Agreement.
Authority Relative to this Agreement. This Agreement constitutes a valid and binding obligation
of (Client) enforceable against it in accordance with its terms.
No Violation. Neither the execution and delivery of this Agreement, nor its performance is
prohibited by, or is a violation of, any contact, agreement, law judgment or court order to which
(Client) is a party or by which (Client) is or may be bound. Nor is the consent or approval of
any person or entity not a party to this Agreement required.
Litigation/Disputes. There is no judicial, administrative or arbitral action, suit or invest igation
or proceeding pending or to (Client’s) knowledge, threatened against it which would prevent
performance of this Agreement.
Valid Issuance. Upon exercise of the Option or any portion thereof, the (Client) shares of Com-
mon Stock will be deemed fully paid and non-assessable and not subject to any encumbranc-
es.
SECTION VII
Confidentiality.
Confidential Information. (Name) acknowledges that during the performance of this Agreement,
(Name) may receive the following confidential information from or relating to (Client) business
strategies, proposed public offerings, acquisitions, spin-offs and other information which (Cli-
ent) stamps as “confidential” (“Confidential Information”). (Name) agrees not to disclose any
Confidential Information, other than to (Name)’s or (Client’s) officers, employees and agents
and, if appropriate, other member of (Client’s) Board or Directors, as necessary to perform the
services required hereunder and as may be required by law or valid legal process.
(Client’s) Materials. All reports, analysis, investment strategies, research materials, trade se-
crets, subscriber lists, computer software, computer records and data stored in “Client’s) com-
puters, computer printouts, computer disks, documents, memoranda, notebooks, correspon-
dence, files, lists and other records, and the like, and all photocopies or other reproductions
thereof, affecting or relating to (Client’s) business which (Name) shall prepare, use, construct,
observe, possess or control(“(Client) Material Is”), Shall be and remain the sole property of
(Client). Upon termination of this Agreement, (Name) or the representative of his estate, shall
promptly deliver to (Client) all such (Client Materials) and any copies or excerpts thereof.
SECTION VIII
Voluntary Termination, Death or Disability of Consultant.
(Name) can terminate his consulting services upon 30 days’ notice to (Client).
“Disability” means incapacity, whether by reason of physical or mental illness or disability,
which prevents Consultant from substantially performing (Name)’s services for four (4) months
in any twelve (12) month period during the term of this Agreement.
Termination for death shall become effective upon the occurrence of such event.
Upon the voluntary termination, death or disability of (Name) as defined herein, the Agreement
shall terminate.
2.
3.
4.
5.
A.
1.
2.
A.
1.
2.
3.
4.

-- 23 of 26 --

Contracts 101 	Page 
There shall be no obligation to pay any Deferred Compensation other than giving (Name) or
his estate a prorate adjustment equal to that portion of his option vested under Section A.1. f.
to the date of death or date of disability of (Name). (Client) shall, however have the option to
cancel the option and pay the portion of the Deferred Compensation due (Name) based on
the increase in value of (Client) from (Date) until the date of voluntary termination or involuntary
termination by death or disability.
Termination for Cause. (Client) may terminate Consultant’s services hereunder for cause (as de-
fined herein) at any time and upon compliance with the provisions set forth in subparagraph C of
this Section VIII, effective upon the failure of Consultant to remedy the deficiencies setforth in the
written notice required by section C below. As used herein, the term “cause” shall mean:
willful and continued failure by Consultant to substantially perform his duties hereunder (other
than resulting from Consultant’s sickness, accident or disability);
a material breach of this Agreement:
violation of any federal or state securities stature, rule or regulation promulgated thereunder:
Violation of any pertinent Federal regulations.
willful criminal misconduct by the Consultant;
dishonesty; or
use of possession of drugs on the premises.
Notice and Ability to Remedy Provisions:
In the event a majority of the officers of (Client) determines that (Name) should be terminated for
cause, (Client) shall furnish and deliver written notice to (Name) setting forth the specific alleged
deficiencies and the factual basis therefore. (Name) shall have 30 days from the date the notice
is received in which to cure the alleged deficiencies. In the event the officers of (Client) determine
that (Name) has not corrected or taken appropriate steps to correct the alleged deficiencies, then
the matter shall be referred to the members of the Board of Directors of (Client), exclusive of
(Name) if he is a director at that time, for a determination as to whether (Name) should be termi-
nated for cause on one of the grounds set forth in B above. If these directors should determine
that (Name) should be discharged for cause, then the date of such determination shall be deemed
the effective date of his termination.
If the Consultant’s services under this Agreement are terminated for Cause, then, as determined by (Client),
the Consultant shall be entitled to exercise 50% of the number of shares which are vested, but not yet paid
for, or 50% of the Deferred Compensation that may be due him on the effective date of his termination for
cause.
Arbitration
In the event any dispute or controversy arises between Makepaece and (Client), such dispute
or controversy shall be arbitrated before the American Arbitration Association (the “AAA”) under
federal arbitration law. In the event Makepaece initiates the arbitration, it shall be at the AAA office
nearest to (Client). In the event (Client) initiates the arbitration, it shall be at the AAA office nearest
the Agency. The prevailing party, as determined by the arbitrator(s), is entitled to all reasonable
legal fees and disbursements incurred by it in the arbitration proceeding or, if necessary, subse-
quent court proceedings. By signing this Agreement, each party recognizes it is giving up the right
5.
B.
1.
2.
3.
4.
5.
6.
7.
C.

-- 24 of 26 --

Contracts 101 	Page 
to file an action in court relating to this Agreement or the transactions contemplated thereby. Any
arbitration award rendered between the parties to this Agreement shall be binding, unless modi-
fied or vacated by a court of competent jurisdiction.
Section X
General Provisions.
Severable Provisions. The provisions of this Agreement are severable, and if anyone or more
provisions may be determined to be judicially unenforceable, in whole or in part, the remaining
provisions shall nevertheless be binding and enforceable.
Assignment. This Agreement may not be assigned by (Name). This Agreement, and the rights
and obligations thereunder, may be assigned by (Client) upon written notice to (Name).
Wavier. Either Party’s failure to enforce any provision or provisions of this Agreement shall not
in any way be construed as a waiver of any such provision or provisions, or prevent that party
thereafter from enforcing each and every other provisions of this Agreement.
Entire Agreement; Amendments. This Agreement supersedes any and all other agreements
between the Parties with the respect to the consulting arrangement described herein. Each
Party to this Agreement acknowledges that no representations inducements, promises or
agreements, orally or otherwise, have been made by any Party, or anyone acting on behalf of
any Party, which are not embodied herein, and that no other agreement, statement or promise
not contained in this Agreement will be effective unless it is in writing signed by the party to be
charged.
Titles and Headings. Titles and headings to sections of this Agreement are for the purpose of
reference only and shall in no way limit, define or otherwise affect the interpretation or con-
struction of such provisions.
Counterparts. This Agreement may be executed in one or more counterparts, each of which
shall be deemed to be and original, and all of which together shall constitute a single agree-
ment.
Governing Law. This Agreement, and its enforcement, shall be governed by and construed in
accordance with the laws of the (Clients) state.
Notices. Any notice to be given (Client) under the terms of this Agreement shall be addressed
to (Client) at the address of (Client) principal place of business at (Address), and any notice
to be given to (Name) shall be addressed to (Name) at (address), or at such other address as
either Party may hereafter designate in writing to the other. Any notice required or permitted
under this Agreement shall be in writing, shall be sent by certified mail, return receipt request-
ed, or by hand, and shall be deemed effective: (I) upon receipt in the event of delivery by hand,
including delivery made by private delivery or overnight mail service where either recipient or
delivery agent executes a written receipt or confirmations of delivery; or (ii) 48 hours after de-
posited in the United States mail, registered or certified mail, return receipt requested, postage
prepaid.
Exhibits. The exhibits annexed hereto and referenced herein are hereby incorporated in and
made a part of this Agreement as if set forth herein. Any matter disclosed in the exhibit an-
nexed hereto shall be deemed also to have been disclosed in the body of this Agreement.
